response: {"purpose":"Figure 3b plots the amplification efficiency (eCT) of each successful eCLIP experiment, with the x-axis showing the eCT of the more efficient replicate (replicate 1) and the y-axis showing the eCT increase in the less efficient replicate (replicate 2), thereby visualising both absolute library yield and inter-replicate consistency across 209 experiments. Seven IgG-only negative-control experiments are overlaid as reference lines spanning the full size range (25–250 kDa in 75 kDa intervals), allowing direct comparison of signal experiments against background. This figure matters because it demonstrates that the eCLIP protocol produces libraries with reproducible amplification requirements across biological replicates and across a large, diverse set of RNA-binding proteins, supporting the scalability and reliability of the method for systematic RBP target identification.","title":"eCLIP Replicate Amplification Efficiency Across 209 Experiments"}
